pi_items.source: H-optimus-0 foundation model for histology (Nature Communications 2025) operates at 0.5 microns per pixel; confirmed as standard resolution for muscle histology image analysis (0.46 μm/pixel in published tongue muscle studies)
Consistent with pixel_scale of 0.5; standard unit for muscle fiber morphometry
Research shows typical exclusion range 200-10000 μm²; 500 is reasonable lower threshold for excluding debris/noise
Published research on muscle fiber morphometry (multiple PMC articles) indicates standard exclusion range of 200-10000 μm², NOT 20000. MyoSight and other automated tools use 10000 as upper limit
Reasonable threshold for successful watershed segmentation; typical muscle cross-section contains many more fibers when properly segmented

Published study on muscle fiber morphometry in histologically normal deltoid muscle: mean CSA 2399.4 ± 106.5 μm² (Springer Nature Link). Upper bound of 8000 is physiologically reasonable for hypertrophied fibers

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic image generated by ImageMagick (fallback when download fails)
pi_items.source: URL in setup_task.sh (https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/c/c5/Skeletal_muscle_-_cross_section_-_HE_stain.jpg) returns HTTP 404 error; image does not exist at that location. Script has fallback: if download fails, ImageMagick generates synthetic hexagonal pattern. Since URL is dead, task always uses synthetic data

privileged_info_summary: The muscle fiber morphometry task uses a pixel scale of 0.5 microns per pixel, which is verified as standard for histological image analysis (confirmed by H-optimus-0 foundation model specification). The expected mean CSA range of 2000-8000 μm² is verified: published research reports mean muscle fiber CSA of 2399.4 ± 106.5 μm² in normal tissue. However, the maximum fiber area threshold of 20000 μm² contradicts published research showing standard maximum of 10000 μm². The claimed image source from Wikimedia Commons is contradicted: the specified URL returns 404 error, indicating the image does not exist; the task setup script therefore uses a synthetic ImageMagick-generated hexagonal pattern as fallback.
